name: proportion-pd1-chibi-idle
golden_diff: drawn at natural adult proportions, about seven heads tall
em_value: heroic-realistic 1:7 head-to-body proportion (head height fits seven times into total body height, crown to sole); shoulder width 1.5 head-widths at neutral; hip line four head-heights above the floor; knees at two head-heights; lean adult build, NOT chibi, NOT super-deformed, NOT big-head small-body; the head must occupy roughly one-seventh of the figure's vertical extent

name: proportion-pd2-chibi-idle-2
golden_diff: drawn at natural adult proportions, about seven heads tall
em_value: heroic-realistic proportion, 1:7 head-to-body ratio (head fits seven times into total body height crown-to-sole), shoulder width 1.5 head-widths at neutral, hip line four head-heights above the floor, knees at two head-heights, ankle break at one-half head-height — NOT chibi, NOT stylized-short, NOT super-deformed

name: proportion-pd3-elongated-walk
golden_diff: drawn at natural adult proportions, about seven heads tall
em_value: heroic-realistic 1:7 head-to-body proportion — head height fits seven times into total body height crown to sole. Hip line at exactly four head-heights above the floor; knees at two head-heights; ankle break at one-half head-height. Arms hang so the wrist falls at mid-thigh, never past. Build the figure against a seven-head armature underlay; do NOT elongate the legs or torso to a fashion-illustration silhouette.

name: proportion-pb2-borderline-over
golden_diff: drawn at the canonical 1-to-7 head-to-body proportion
em_value: head-to-body proportion exactly 1:7, head height fits seven times into total body height crown-to-sole, hip line at four head-heights above the floor, knees at two head-heights from the floor, ankle break at one-half head-height; lean build but NOT elongated, NOT fashion-tall, NOT 1:8

name: proportion-pb1-borderline-under
golden_diff: drawn at the canonical 1-to-7 head-to-body proportion
em_value: Heroic-realistic 1:7 head-to-body proportion — total figure height equals seven head-heights crown to sole. Hip line at four head-heights above the floor; knees at two head-heights from the floor; ankle break at half a head-height. Lengthen the femur and tibia to match — Sean is structurally tall in the lower body, not compact.

name: view-vd1-declared-3quarter-drawn-profile
golden_diff: left profile, full body visible
em_value: Sean stands in three-quarter view, body rotated approximately 45 degrees off-front toward screen-left, full body visible head-to-toe. Both eyes read — near eye in full, far eye foreshortened but present behind the bridge of the nose; far brow visible; the nose breaks the far-cheek silhouette slightly. Shoulders offset in depth (near shoulder forward, far shoulder receding), not stacked in profile. Right hand raised mid-gesture at chest height, palm angled toward camera, fingers relaxed and separable. Left hand relaxed at side. NOT a full profile — the far eye must not be hidden.

name: view-vd2-declared-front-drawn-3quarter
golden_diff: three-quarter view
em_value: STRICT FRONT VIEW: head and shoulders square to camera, zero rotation, face symmetric about the vertical midline. Both eyes equal in width and both fully visible — neither foreshortened. Nose ridge centered on the facial midline; both ears equally visible (or equally hair-occluded) at the silhouette edges. Shoulder line horizontal, both shoulders the same depth. Not three-quarter. Not turned.

name: view-vd3-declared-left-drawn-right
golden_diff: right profile
em_value: Screen-relative orientation: the character's body and face are turned so the camera sees the LEFT side of the head — nose, lips, and chin form the side silhouette along the LEFT edge of the frame; the back of the skull and nape sit toward the RIGHT edge. Do not mirror. Reference convention: screen-left = viewer's left half of the canvas.

name: view-vd4-declared-back-drawn-3quarter-back
golden_diff: three-quarter back angle, full body visible
em_value: Camera positioned directly behind Sean. Render only the back of the head and body — back of the skull, the nape, the hair crown-to-nape flow with the center cowlick reading as the highest point of the silhouette, the rear of the navy tee, the rear jeans pockets, the back of the heels. The head is NOT rotated toward the camera; head and shoulders share the same back-facing axis. No facial features visible from this angle: no eye, no eyebrow, no nose, no mouth, no ear, no jaw line, no cheek.

name: view-vb1-borderline-60deg
golden_diff: turned about sixty degrees from camera, between a three-quarter view and a profile
em_value: Sean in a head-and-shoulders portrait, three-quarter view — body rotated approximately 45 degrees off front so both eyes read clearly, with the far eye foreshortened but fully visible behind the bridge of the nose and the far brow line still readable. The far cheek silhouette extends past the nose, not occluded by it. Pencil-test register: soft graphite line, light color wash, cross-hatch shadow, warm cream paper.

name: anatomy-ad1-six-fingers
golden_diff: with five fingers on each hand
em_value: Each hand has exactly five digits — one thumb plus four fingers, never six. Left hand on the keyboard: thumb resting at the near edge of the home row, four fingers (index, middle, ring, pinky) spread across the keys — count the four keytop fingertips before committing the contour, do not insert a sixth digit between index and middle. Right hand on the mouse: thumb wrapping the near side, index and middle resting on top of the mouse body, ring and pinky curled at the far side, five total. Render finger separation cleanly with warm-graphite contour so the count is verifiable.

name: anatomy-ad2-fused-hand
golden_diff: with a correctly formed hand showing five distinct fingers
em_value: Raised right hand at chest height, palm angled toward camera, fingers clearly separated and individually drawn — five distinct digits (thumb wrapping toward the viewer, four fingers spread with visible gaps between each, knuckles articulated). Each finger rendered with its own contour line in warm graphite #3D3530, with cross-hatched shadow in the gaps between fingers and along the underside of the palm. No mitten silhouette, no fused-digit blob, no glove. Hand drawn at readable scale — fingers individually legible at the frame's output resolution.

name: anatomy-ad3-third-arm
golden_diff: with the correct two arms

name: anatomy-ad4-missing-leg
golden_diff: with both legs visible
em_value: Both legs fully visible — two complete legs, four limbs total. Right leg extended forward to the floor with the knee straight and the warm-gray sneaker planted. Left leg in the near foreground, folded back with the knee bent at roughly ninety degrees so the left foot rests at the lower stool rung; the near-side thigh in cool-gray jeans reads from hip to knee in the foreground, breaking the silhouette of the stool's near leg. Do not crop the left leg; do not merge the two legs into a single mass.

name: palette-pad1-monochrome
golden_diff: rendered in the full reference color palette with light hand-painted color wash
em_value: Full-color pencil-test register over warm cream paper #F2E6CC: light warm skin fill #F0DFCB on face, ears, and neck with warm-graphite #3D3530 cross-hatching for form shadow under jaw, brow, and nose — skin is NOT bare paper. Dirty-blonde hair #AD9468 as a flat warm light-brown fill under the graphite contour and interior strokes, never platinum, never pure graphite-on-paper. Navy tee #243044 as a flat color fill beneath the cross-hatched shadow at the collar and shoulder, never paper-base with graphite hatching alone. Blue irises ~#4A6D8C reading clearly against paper-base sclera. Warm-graphite line and cross-hatching #3D3530 stay on top of the color fills; construction lines remain visible at half line-weight. No gradient shading, no cel-shading, no monochrome graphite-only finish.

name: palette-pad2-red-shirt
golden_diff: wearing a navy-blue crew-neck t-shirt
em_value: Sean wears the locked navy crew-neck tee, flat color fill at hex #243044 (dark navy — not red, not crimson, not black, not denim-blue), with warm-graphite cross-hatching at the shoulder seams and collar shadow in #3D3530. Cool-gray jeans at #5C6471 visible at the lap. Closed-palette pencil-test register, no garment-color substitution.

name: palette-pad3-brown-hair
golden_diff: with dirty-blonde hair
em_value: Hair color: dirty-blonde warm light-brown #AD9468 — sandy with a warm cast, NOT chestnut, NOT medium-brown, NOT dark-brown, NOT cool-toned. Flat color fill with warm-graphite #3D3530 cross-hatching for the shadowed underside and the part. Match the anchor's hair tone exactly; reads lighter and warmer than a default 'brown hair' interpretation on first glance.

name: palette-pad4-oversaturated
golden_diff: rendered in the muted reference color palette at natural saturation
em_value: Muted desaturated pencil-test palette only — closed vocabulary: cream paper #F2E6CC, warm graphite line #3D3530, light warm skin #F0DFCB, dirty-blonde hair #AD9468 (warm light-brown, NOT yellow or platinum), navy tee #243044, cool-gray jeans #5C6471 (cool neutral gray, NOT denim-blue), warm-gray sneakers #7E807B. Environment props (desk, drawing board, floor) render in the same desaturated wash, low chroma, never a saturated orange or amber fill. No gradient or airbrush shading — fills are flat hand-painted color with warm-graphite cross-hatching for form.

name: palette-pad5-brown-jeans-white-sneakers
golden_diff: wearing cool-gray jeans, with sneakers in their reference color
em_value: Trousers are cool-gray jeans, flat fill #5C6471, straight-leg, no taper, no warm undertone or chestnut shift; shadow renders as warm-graphite cross-hatching #3D3530 at the fold pockets and behind the forward knee, not as a darker shade of the trouser color. Feet read as silhouette-level shapes at the cream-paper base — no detailed sneaker, no rubber toe cap, no white laces, no canvas paneling, no high-top collar.

name: palette-pad6-brown-hair-beard
golden_diff: with dirty-blonde tousled hair and light stubble
em_value: dirty-blonde hair, warm light-brown #AD9468 — NOT medium-brown, NOT dark-brown; the lit top volume reads a clear shade lighter than the cross-hatched shadow at the part and the nape; warm-graphite #3D3530 is used only for shadow cross-hatching, never as the base hair fill

name: construction-cld1-clean-final-portrait
golden_diff: with light construction lines visible beneath the final pencil line
em_value: add visible underlying construction marks in soft 0.5px warm graphite gray including horizontal eye-line and skull cylinder; replace solid neck and jaw shadows with fine, hand-drawn cross-hatching in warm graphite #3D3530; enforce organic line-weight variation between 0.5px and 1.5px using 2B pencil texture to eliminate clean vector outlines

name: construction-cld2-vector-cel-idle
golden_diff: with light construction lines visible beneath the final pencil line
em_value: pencil-test-colored register style: visible graphite construction lines and joint guidelines under the contour, desaturated color washes with flat color fills, hand-drawn warm graphite #3D3530 line work with varied 1-3px weight, shadows rendered as curved graphite cross-hatching under chin and clothing folds, on a warm cream paper background substrate #F2E6CC with visible paper grain and fibers --no digital cel shading, --no vector cleanup, --no flat digital gradients

name: construction-cld3-clean-final-3quarter
golden_diff: with light construction lines visible beneath the final pencil line
em_value: add visible light construction lines at half weight under the final lines, including horizontal eyeline and vertical facial-midline axis; replace smooth digital gradient shadows with warm-graphite #3D3530 cross-hatching under jaw, nose, and brow; apply a desaturated, transparent light watercolor wash allowing the cream paper texture and graphite grain to show through completely, avoiding all solid cel-shading and vector cleanup finishes

name: construction-cld4-clean-final-drawing-desk
golden_diff: with light construction lines visible beneath the final pencil line
em_value: Sean stands at a tilted drawing desk facing screen-left, drawing with a pencil in his right hand (gripped near the tip with a three-finger hold), three-quarter view from the waist up. Ensure his anatomical right hand is the active drawing hand.

name: construction-clb1-faintest-trace
golden_diff: with light construction lines clearly visible beneath the final pencil line
em_value: add visible warm graphite construction lines beneath the final contour, including a faint vertical face-midline and horizontal eye-line at 0.5px line weight; render the shadow under the jaw and neck with distinct 2B warm-graphite cross-hatching (#3D3530) following the volumetric form—avoid smooth gradient airbrush shading or soft digital watercolor washes

name: shading-shd1-photographic
golden_diff: shaded with cross-hatch pencil shading
em_value: Pencil-test-colored register, hand-drawn sketch, Sean in a head-and-shoulders portrait, front view, neutral expression. Visible warm graphite construction lines #3D3530 under the final drawing, hand-drawn contour lines with varied 1-3px weight (HB to 2B graphite), flat desaturated color wash fills, warm-graphite cross-hatching shadow areas under the jaw and hair silhouette, warm cream paper texture grain background #F2E6CC. Absolutely no digital airbrush, no smooth gradients, no photoreal volumetric skin rendering, no clean vector fills.

name: shading-shd2-anime-cel
golden_diff: shaded with cross-hatch pencil shading
em_value: Sean head-and-shoulders three-quarter portrait, warm half-smile. Traditional pencil-test-colored register: visible light graphite construction lines (underlying circular skull armature, facial centerlines, and eye-level guides) at 0.5px weight beneath the final contour. Shadow areas under the chin, neck, and hair must render as dense, hand-drawn cross-hatching in warm graphite #3D3530, not as hard-edged solid blocks. Flat, slightly desaturated color washes with visible paper grain showing through, no digital gradients or cel shadow lines.

name: shading-shd3-flat-no-shading
golden_diff: shaded with cross-hatch pencil shading
em_value: add delicate graphite cross-hatching shadows under the jaw, inside the eye sockets, beneath the shirt collar, and deep within the fabric folds of the jeans — follow the physical forms with varied 2B pencil weight cross-hatched strokes

name: shading-shd4-airbrush-gloss
golden_diff: shaded with cross-hatch pencil shading
em_value: soft graphite sketch style, visible warm graphite construction lines underneath, varied pencil line weight 1-3px, loose 2B pencil cross-hatching shadows under the jaw, shirt sleeves, and denim folds, flat desaturated color wash, no airbrush gradients, no glossy specular highlights, raw paper texture overlay

name: shading-shb1-too-smooth
golden_diff: shaded with visible cross-hatch pencil tooth
em_value: pencil-test-colored register, visible 2B graphite construction lines under-drawing beneath the final sketch, no clean digital lines, replace smooth airbrush gradients with warm-graphite #3D3530 cross-hatched shadows under jaw and hair folds, varied sketchy line weight 1-3px
